text: Both spaces are dimly lit, nearly empty, and filled with an eerie quiet. There is an almost clinical sterility to the environment, broken only by the subdued glow of screens and emergency lights. The silence is palpable, and I feel as though I am wandering through the abandoned after-hours of a place meant for bustling activity and intellectual fervor. I am struck by a sense of anticipation, as if the ghost of purpose still haunts these corridors. It is a moment of solitude that invites contemplation about the purpose of spaces when stripped of their people and their noise.
